judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong recent earnings beat, but trades at a high valuation with significant debt and low profit margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 242.4%; next earnings date 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 242.4% — significant positive surprise.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for current Q (286.1%) and FY (179.5%).
Strong liquidity with Current ratio 5.03 and Quick ratio 3.98.
Consensus analyst rating is 'buy' with a high number of outperform ratings.
negatives: Forward P/E 75.70x and Price/Sales 36.89x — extremely high valuation multiples.
Profit margin 0.2% and Operating margin 9.6% — profitability is very thin.
Return on equity 1.3% — low profitability relative to shareholder equity.
Debt/Equity ratio 311.48 — high leverage on the balance sheet.
Mean analyst target $245.13 is below the current spot price of $317.25.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) is being highlighted as a potential top climate change stock, with recent analyst reports and insider interest focusing on its position in the sector. While the company has shown significant revenue growth and a strong earnings surprise last quarter, its high valuation multiples and substantial debt warrant consideration. The upcoming earnings report will be key to watch for continued growth acceleration and management's outlook on achieving profitability.

headlines.title: Is Bloom Energy (BE) One of the Best Climate Change Stocks to Buy According to Analysts?
